c.d: Percentage of target audience already using the app. Higher adoption reduces the barrier to entry for new group members.
c.s: App user statistics, market analysis reports

c.d: Maximum number of recipients allowed in a single broadcast message.
Availability and sophistication of personalization options (e.g., inserting recipient names, custom fields).
Ability to schedule broadcast messages for delivery at a future time.
The cost associated with sending a single broadcast message. This is particularly relevant for apps that charge per message.
Whether the app requires explicit consent from recipients before sending broadcast messages to comply with regulations.
Types of media supported in broadcast messages (e.g., images, videos, documents).
Availability of automated responses, drip campaigns, and other automated messaging features.
Seamless integration with other business or productivity tools (e.g., CRM, marketing platforms).
Level of encryption and privacy features offered by the app.
